questions.q: Shatrughna is the youngest of the four sons of which king of Ayodhya?
questions.explanation: Shatrughna was the youngest of the four sons born to King Dasharatha of Ayodhya.

questions.q: Shatrughna is the twin brother of which of his siblings?
questions.explanation: Shatrughna and Lakshmana were twins, both born to Queen Sumitra.

questions.q: Shatrughna was the constant companion and devoted attendant of which brother?
questions.explanation: Just as Lakshmana served Rama, Shatrughna was the constant companion of Bharata.

questions.q: What is the meaning of the name 'Shatrughna'?
questions.explanation: The name Shatrughna literally means 'slayer of enemies' or 'vanquisher of foes'.

questions.q: Which powerful demon did Shatrughna later slay?
questions.explanation: Shatrughna is renowned for slaying the demon Lavanasura.

questions.q: Which city is Shatrughna traditionally credited with founding?
questions.explanation: After slaying Lavanasura, Shatrughna founded the city of Mathura on the site of the demon's domain.

questions.q: Who was the mother of Shatrughna?
questions.explanation: Shatrughna, along with his twin Lakshmana, was born to Queen Sumitra.

questions.q: How many sons did King Dasharatha have in total, including Shatrughna?
questions.explanation: Dasharatha had four sons: Rama, Bharata, Lakshmana, and Shatrughna.

questions.q: Which of these is the eldest brother whom Shatrughna and the others revered?
questions.explanation: Rama was the eldest of the four brothers and was revered by all of them.

questions.q: In which great Hindu epic does Shatrughna appear as a central figure?
questions.explanation: Shatrughna is one of the four brothers of the Ramayana, the epic composed by Valmiki.

questions.q: Shatrughna was married to Shrutakirti, who was related to Sita as her what?
questions.explanation: Shrutakirti, Shatrughna's wife, was Sita's cousin, being the daughter of Kushadhvaja, brother of King Janaka.

questions.q: Whose father was Kushadhvaja, the father of Shatrughna's bride Shrutakirti?
options: He was the brother of Janaka
He was the brother of Dasharatha
He was the brother of Vishvamitra
He was the brother of Sumitra
questions.explanation: Kushadhvaja was the younger brother of King Janaka of Mithila, and his daughter Shrutakirti married Shatrughna.

questions.q: When Bharata journeyed to Chitrakuta to plead with Rama to return, who accompanied him?
questions.explanation: Shatrughna accompanied Bharata on the journey to Chitrakuta to entreat Rama to return to Ayodhya.

questions.q: The demon Lavanasura whom Shatrughna slew was the son of which demoness?
questions.explanation: Lavanasura was the son of the demoness Kumbhini and the demon Madhu.

questions.q: Lavanasura possessed a fearsome divine weapon given by Shiva. What was it?
questions.explanation: Lavanasura wielded a powerful trident (shula) given to his father Madhu by Lord Shiva, which Shatrughna had to avoid in battle.

questions.q: How did Shatrughna manage to defeat the otherwise invincible Lavanasura?
options: By attacking when Lavanasura was without his trident
By using Brahma's weapon at night
By tricking him into the river
questions.explanation: Shatrughna attacked Lavanasura when the demon had left his temple without his all-powerful trident, allowing the hero to slay him.

questions.q: Which divine weapon did Rama give to Shatrughna to use against Lavanasura?
questions.explanation: Rama gave Shatrughna a powerful celestial arrow associated with Vishnu to slay the demon Lavanasura.

questions.q: During Rama's coronation as king of Ayodhya, what role did Shatrughna and his brothers play?
options: They stood as devoted attendants and rulers
questions.explanation: At Rama's coronation, the brothers including Shatrughna served devotedly and shared in the governance of the kingdom.

questions.q: While Bharata ruled Ayodhya as regent during Rama's exile, where did Bharata reside in austerity?
questions.explanation: Bharata governed from Nandigrama while keeping Rama's sandals on the throne, and Shatrughna remained at his side.

questions.q: Shatrughna is noted for once punishing which figure who had instigated the events leading to Rama's exile?
questions.explanation: In the Ramayana, Shatrughna, enraged, seized and punished Manthara, the hunchbacked maid who had incited Kaikeyi.

questions.q: Together with which brother is Shatrughna often paired as a devoted secondary brother in the epic?
questions.explanation: Shatrughna is paired with Bharata, mirroring how Lakshmana is paired with Rama.

questions.q: After founding his capital, Shatrughna ruled over the region associated with which river?
questions.explanation: Mathura, the city Shatrughna founded, stands on the banks of the Yamuna river.

questions.q: According to the Uttara Kanda, how many sons did Shatrughna have who were given kingdoms?
questions.explanation: Shatrughna had two sons, Subahu and Shatrughati (also called Shurasena), who were established as rulers.

questions.q: What was the name of the elder son of Shatrughna who succeeded him at Mathura?
questions.explanation: Subahu was Shatrughna's elder son, who succeeded him in the rule of Mathura.

questions.q: The demon Madhu, father of Lavanasura, gave his name to which forest where Mathura was later built?
questions.explanation: Madhu ruled the forest called Madhuvana, where Shatrughna later established the city of Mathura.

questions.q: On his march to confront Lavanasura, Shatrughna stayed at the hermitage of which sage where Sita's sons were being raised?
questions.explanation: Shatrughna lodged at the ashram of the sage Valmiki, where Lava and Kusha, the sons of Rama, were being raised.

questions.q: For approximately how long does the Ramayana say Shatrughna ruled at Mathura before returning to Ayodhya?
questions.explanation: The Uttara Kanda relates that Shatrughna governed Mathura for about twelve years before visiting Rama in Ayodhya.

questions.q: Which sage advised Rama that Lavanasura must be slain because the demon menaced the holy hermitages?
questions.explanation: The sage Chyavana came to Rama's court describing the menace of Lavanasura, prompting Shatrughna's mission.

questions.q: In the Uttara Kanda's account of the brothers' departure from the world, what did Shatrughna do after Rama prepared to leave?
options: He installed his sons and followed Rama in renunciation
He was crowned emperor of all India
questions.explanation: Shatrughna installed his two sons as kings of Mathura and then joined Rama in the final renunciation.

questions.q: Shatrughna and Lakshmana, both sons of Sumitra, are traditionally regarded as partial incarnations connected with which divine attribute of Vishnu?
options: His conch and discus (Shankha and Chakra)
questions.explanation: Tradition holds that Lakshmana and Shatrughna embody portions linked to Vishnu's serpent and emblems such as the conch and discus, serving Rama who is Vishnu incarnate.
